summary: The static checks failed due to TypeScript/type-checking errors: the log reports 118 errors (97 unique) across multiple TS/TSX and locale files, causing the CI job to fail.
root_cause: Type checker reported 118 errors (97 unique) in the repository, originating from multiple TypeScript/TSX and localization files (e.g., src/utils/errorHandling.ts, src/pages/Dashboard/Stats/DAGImportErrors.tsx).

evidence.reason: The log summary explicitly reports that 118 errors were found, indicating the static check/type-check stage failed.
Shows that the following log entries are errors (not just warnings or info).
This TypeScript file is listed among the error locations, indicating a type/compile issue in source code.
A TSX file is shown in the error list, supporting that TypeScript/type-checking errors are present.
The log also contains many warnings alongside errors, showing the static checks reported multiple issues of different severities.

suggested_fix: Reproduce the static/type-check locally and fix the TypeScript errors reported in the log. Start by running the project's static checks or type checker locally, inspect the full error output for files such as src/utils/errorHandling.ts and src/pages/Dashboard/Stats/DAGImportErrors.tsx, and correct the types/code causing the failures.

summary: The CI job emitted thousands of /usr/bin/tar errors while operating on files under the Go module cache (go/pkg/mod), which caused the run to fail.
root_cause: The archive step using /usr/bin/tar produced many errors when trying to process files under '../../../go/pkg/mod' (Go module cache), leading to job failure.

evidence.reason: Shows a very large number of errors were reported, indicating a systemic failure rather than a single test failure.
Direct evidence that /usr/bin/tar failed while operating on paths under the Go module cache (go/pkg/mod).
Additional tar error messages referencing go/pkg/mod entries, showing the problem affected many module paths.

suggested_fix: Inspect and fix the CI step that runs /usr/bin/tar on '../../../go/pkg/mod': check tar command arguments, file permissions, symlinks, and path handling. Consider excluding or correctly referencing the Go module cache when creating archives/caches, or adjust workspace permissions so tar can read those paths.

summary: The CI job failed during the build step: numerous g++/gcc compilation/linking errors occurred causing the build to abort.
root_cause: C++ compilation/linking failed during the build (multiple g++/gcc commands reported errors), causing the job to fail.

evidence.reason: Shows many errors occurred during the run, indicating a build failure rather than a transient test flake.
Repeated g++ invocation lines show compilation/linking steps failed.
gcc invocation also failed, indicating multiple C/C++ compile/link commands errored.
The build was configured with verbose and error-on-warn, showing the subsequent compile errors happened after configure.
Presence of stderr marker indicates there are error messages from the build toolchain captured in the log.
An addon build step was executed and likely contributed to the compiler errors reported.

suggested_fix: Open the full CI log around the first reported stderr/g++ failure (see the '##[error]--- stderr ---' section) to identify the exact compiler error messages; fix the underlying C/C++ compilation or linking errors (e.g., missing symbols, include paths, or incompatible flags) and re-run the build. If warnings are treated as errors due to '--error-on-warn', consider addressing or temporarily disabling those warnings while fixing the root cause.

summary: Meson/compile checks failed during the build: test compile/link commands produced errors (missing headers, pragma diagnostics turned into errors, and linker failures), causing the CI job to fail before tests ran.
root_cause: Meson attempted to compile/check small programs and compilation failed with missing-header errors and diagnostic-as-error pragmas (e.g. missing 'version' and 'sys/mman.h' headers and '-Wattributes' promoted to error), leading to linker failures and aborting the build.

evidence.reason: Shows the configure/compile check failed because a required header 'version' was not found.
Shows another missing system header detected during compilation checks.
Indicates that attribute-related warnings are being treated as errors, which will turn warnings into hard compilation failures.

evidence.quote: collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
evidence.reason: Shows a linker error occurred during compilation of a test/program, causing the build to fail.
Demonstrates that the C compiler was invoked by Meson to build test programs and that the failure happened during those invocations.

suggested_fix: Install or make available the missing system/development headers (e.g. provide headers like sys/mman.h and any 'version' header) and/or adjust the build/meson configuration so attribute warnings (e.g. -Wattributes) are not promoted to errors; then re-run the build.

evidence.reason: Additional submodule-related warning supporting the presence of submodule/config issues in the log.

suggested_fix: Investigate and resolve the git submodule config lock (inspect .git/modules/library/backtrace config/lock and fix permissions or remove stale lock), then re-run the test job to get the concrete failing test output and address individual test failures.
